title: No item-level read at tool surface — read-block is all-or-nothing; no read-block-item / paginated read; large blocks force whole-file fetch
description: read-block returns the entire block's JSON. Large blocks (decisions ~109KB, framework-gaps growing similarly) force whole-file fetch even when the caller wants one item. resolve-item-by-id covers by-ID lookup; filter-block-items covers predicate filter; but there's no read-block-item(block, id) for direct item fetch by ID without the block roundtrip, and no paginated read for blocks too large to inject. Distinct from FGAP-014 (pagination in renderers like renderLensView / renderRoadmap) and FGAP-032 (item-level INJECTION at JIT-agent dispatch via contextBlocks selectivity). This is item-level READ at the tool API surface for harness-confined main LLM consumption.

evidence.reference: read-block tool returns whole block via readBlock from block-api; no item-by-id variant + no paginated variant
FGAP-014 (renderer pagination) + FGAP-032 (item-level contextBlocks injection at dispatch) are adjacent but distinct — neither covers tool-API item-level read
DEC-0014 — context-window economy under harness confinement makes whole-block-fetch-when-one-item-wanted a substrate-surface inefficiency

impact: Context-window cost when caller wants one item from a large block. read-block-item via filter-block-items + take(1) requires two tool calls (filter then implicit pick); resolve-item-by-id covers when the ID is known but not when discovery + read should compose. No paginated read means large blocks can exceed the truncateHead 50KB threshold (already observed; truncation message surfaces). Under growing substrate this gap compounds.
canonical_vocabulary: item-level read at tool surface; paginated block-read
proposed_resolution: (1) Add read-block-item(cwd, block, id) tool: equivalent to filter-block-items(block, id, eq) + take(1) but as a single primitive returning the item OR null. (2) Add read-block-page(cwd, block, offset, limit) for paginated reads of large blocks; returns `{items: ItemRecord[], total: number, hasMore: boolean}`. (3) read-block stays for full-block fetches; truncateHead behavior unchanged. Library + pi tool + orchestrator script per DEC-0019 dual-surface.
